behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is parser logic at a single function/object boundary (segmenting a command string). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocks do not hide this because pure input->output parsing can be tested directly. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration/System are disqualified by behavior text "Command segmentation..." (pure segmentation rule, no cross-module infra requirement). Demotion below Agentic is supported by deterministic plumbing text "newline-separated statements" and absence of any AI/ML output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative multiline shell strings into the segmentation function and assert segment arrays (assignments and later command are separate entries), including whitespace/newline edge cases.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: incident occurred at module handoff (segmentation/classification across multiline input), so unit-only classifier tests can miss it. REAL-INFRA (integration-brake a): no real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce classification bug. LLM-DEP (b): no AI/ML output dependency; behavior is deterministic trigger detection. MULTI-STEP (c,d): no agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: System disqualified because behavior text focuses on recognition logic "recognized as a PR-create trigger" for a shell block, not real `gh` execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual post-tool trigger-detection pipeline (segmenter + trigger classifier) on a multiline block with assignments then `gh pr create`; assert result is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: false-positive prevention depends on same cross-module parsing+classification boundary as behavior 2. REAL-INFRA (integration-brake a): no real subprocess/network needed; deterministic local pipeline is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP (b,c,d): none. Rejection-gate: System disqualified by behavior text "not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger" (classification scope only). Demotion below Agentic justified by deterministic command matching text "gh issue create" and no model-content evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real detection pipeline on multiline input ending with `gh issue create`; assert classification is not PR-create trigger and does not open review loop state.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is handoff from trigger detection/output parsing to state persistence (multiple modules plus storage boundary). REAL-INFRA (integration-brake a): local filesystem write/read in test workspace is enough; no real network or subprocess required to catch this bug class. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP (b,c,d): none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is about "reconstruct and persist" identifier, not real `gh` call behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke post-tool hook flow with a detected PR-create event payload containing PR metadata/output; assert PR identifier is reconstructed correctly and persisted to the expected state artifact with required fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit inter-module contract validation (writer hook -> reader/enforcer hook). Unit tests of either side alone can pass while contract breaks. REAL-INFRA (integration-brake a): no external OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local file I/O. LLM-DEP (b): none; deterministic schema/contract check. MULTI-STEP (c,d): no agentic stages. Rejection-gate: System disqualified because behavior text is contract compatibility "can read and enforce," not real external process interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run post-tool write path to produce gate state, then run pre-tool enforcement read/validate path against that artifact; assert enforcement activates based on persisted state and expected schema/version fields.
